questions.q: Whose devoted brother is Lakshmana, accompanying him into exile?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana is the devoted brother of Rama and followed him into the forest exile.

questions.q: Who was the father of Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana was one of the four sons of King Dasharatha of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Who was the mother of Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana was born to Sumitra, the third queen of Dasharatha.

questions.q: Which kingdom was Lakshmana a prince of?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana was a prince of the kingdom of Ayodhya, ruled by Dasharatha.

questions.q: How many years of exile did Lakshmana share with Rama and Sita?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana accompanied Rama and Sita for the full fourteen years of forest exile.

questions.q: Whom did Lakshmana serve and protect alongside Rama during the exile?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana tirelessly served and guarded both Rama and Sita throughout the exile.

questions.q: Which weapon is Lakshmana most associated with as a warrior?
questions.explanation: Like Rama, Lakshmana was a master archer skilled with the bow and arrow.

questions.q: Lakshmana is often described as which figure in relation to Rama?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana is famously called 'Rama's shadow' for his constant, devoted companionship.

questions.q: To which broad class of beings did the foes Lakshmana battled, like Ravana's kin, belong?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana fought many rakshasas (demons), the race to which Ravana and his family belonged.

questions.q: Lakshmana chose to live in the forest rather than remain comfortably where?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana gave up the comforts of the Ayodhya palace to serve Rama in exile.

questions.q: Who was the wife of Lakshmana, left behind in Ayodhya during the exile?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana married Urmila, Sita's sister, who remained in Ayodhya during his exile.

questions.q: Which rakshasa prince, son of Ravana, did Lakshmana ultimately slay?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana killed Indrajit, also called Meghanada, Ravana's mightiest son.

questions.q: Lakshmana was the twin brother of which of Dasharatha's sons, both born to Sumitra?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana and Shatrughna were the twin sons of Sumitra.

questions.q: Which demoness did Lakshmana punish by cutting off her nose and ears at Panchavati?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana mutilated Shurpanakha, Ravana's sister, after she threatened Sita.

questions.q: What protective boundary did Lakshmana draw around the hut before leaving Sita to find Rama?
questions.explanation: By tradition Lakshmana drew the Lakshmana Rekha, a protective line Sita was not to cross.

questions.q: When Bharata came to the forest with an army, what did Lakshmana initially suspect?
options: That Bharata came to attack Rama
That Bharata was a demon in disguise
That Bharata wished to join the exile
questions.explanation: Lakshmana grew angry, suspecting Bharata had come with the army to harm Rama, until Rama calmed him.

questions.q: After Ravana abducted Sita, whom did Rama and Lakshmana befriend as an ally in the search?
questions.explanation: Rama and Lakshmana allied with the monkey-king Sugriva to help find and rescue Sita.

questions.q: Lakshmana was an incarnation (amsha) of which divine being in tradition?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds Lakshmana to be an incarnation of Shesha, the serpent on whom Vishnu reclines.

questions.q: Who tricked Lakshmana into leaving Sita's side by imitating Rama's cry for help?
questions.explanation: The demon Maricha, as the golden deer, mimicked Rama's voice, luring Lakshmana away so Ravana could seize Sita.

questions.q: During the war, when Lakshmana fell gravely wounded, what was needed to save him?
options: The Sanjivani herb from a mountain
questions.explanation: Hanuman fetched the life-restoring Sanjivani herb (from Mount Dronagiri) to heal the wounded Lakshmana.

questions.q: Which loyal devotee flew to bring the healing herb when Lakshmana lay near death?
questions.explanation: Hanuman carried the entire mountain bearing the Sanjivani herb to revive Lakshmana.

questions.q: According to tradition, what penance allowed Lakshmana to remain awake during the exile, earning him the name Gudakesha?
options: He conquered sleep, giving his share to his wife Urmila
He stood on one leg for years
questions.explanation: Tradition says Lakshmana conquered sleep so as to guard Rama and Sita, and Urmila slept on his behalf.

questions.q: Why was Lakshmana able to kill Indrajit when others could not, according to tradition?
options: He used a weapon from Agni
Indrajit could only be slain by one who had conquered sleep and fasted
questions.explanation: Tradition holds Indrajit could be killed only by a man who had not slept or eaten for fourteen years, conditions Lakshmana fulfilled.

questions.q: At which sacred site did Lakshmana sever Indrajit's connection to his power before slaying him?
options: Nikumbhila, where Indrajit performed his secret sacrifice
questions.explanation: Lakshmana attacked Indrajit at Nikumbhila, interrupting the rite that would have made him invincible.

questions.q: When Rama in despair spoke of giving up after Sita's abduction, what role did Lakshmana take?
options: He left to find Sita alone
He counseled and steadied Rama, urging him to act
He returned to Ayodhya for help
questions.explanation: Lakshmana repeatedly encouraged and steadied the grieving Rama, urging resolute action.

questions.q: In the Uttara Kanda, what ultimately led to Lakshmana's death after the return to Ayodhya?
options: He died in a later battle
Rama was bound to renounce him after he interrupted a fateful meeting with Yama
He was cursed by a sage
He drowned in the Sarayu in battle
questions.explanation: When Lakshmana interrupted Rama's private meeting with Time/Yama, Rama was bound by his word to renounce him, and Lakshmana then gave up his life.

questions.q: Which demon, encountered early in the exile, seized Rama and Lakshmana in his long arms before they freed themselves?
questions.explanation: The rakshasa Viradha seized the brothers in the Dandaka forest; they slew him and freed themselves.

questions.q: Whom did Lakshmana sternly rebuke and threaten when an ally delayed mustering forces to search for Sita?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana confronted Sugriva for indulging in pleasures and neglecting his promise to help find Sita.

questions.q: According to tradition, when Lakshmana could not look upon Sita's ornaments, which jewelry could he reliably identify?
questions.explanation: Out of pure reverence Lakshmana never raised his eyes above Sita's feet, so he recognized only her anklets.

questions.q: After Rama's coronation, which kingdom is Lakshmana said in tradition to have had his sons rule?
options: Angada and Chandraketu's realms (e.g., near the Himalayas)
questions.explanation: Tradition holds Lakshmana's sons Angada and Chandraketu were established as rulers of territories such as Karupada and Chandrakanta.
